Title: Metabolic engineering of folate and its precursors in Mexican common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris L.)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Pinto Saltillo (1424-FRI-026-120901/C); Pinto Café (wild variety); Pinto Durango (PT91082)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first committed step of the pteridine branch from GTP toward folate biosynthesis, increasing biosynthetic pteridine production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential vitamin B9 for human nutrition; deficiency is linked to neural tube defects and other health problems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of folate and strongly increases pteridine precursors in bean seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed-specific expression of AtGchI in common bean significantly increased total biosynthetic pteridines, up to about 150-fold in hyperaccumulating lines. 2. This precursor boost elevated total folate in desiccated seeds by up to 3.3-fold, reaching 325 µg/100 g in the best line. 3. AtGchI expression unexpectedly also increased PABA pools in many lines, suggesting altered folate homeostasis in bean seeds. 4. Pteridine overproduction did not always correlate proportionally with folate, indicating downstream bottlenecks in folate synthesis or salvage/storage.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in transformed seeds under a seed-specific β-conglycinin promoter; detected in T3 seeds by RT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation by particle bombardment; PCR screening across generations; seed RT-PCR; metabolite profiling of pteridines, folates and PABA; PABA feeding assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme; supported as a key control point for boosting the pteridine precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; seed desiccation; PABA imbibition treatment (0.2 mM, 12 h) for selected lines
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Large pteridine accumulation with only modest folate gains indicates downstream bottlenecks; considerable oxidized pteridine accumulation suggests folate degradation/turnover during seed desiccation; no obvious pod or seed number penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful first-gene entry point for folate biofortification in bean seed. Best deployed in multigene stacking with PABA-pathway enhancement and likely downstream pull/stabilization modules such as FPGS or folate-binding/sequestration strategies to overcome bottlenecks and degradation during seed maturation/storage.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes that bean seeds appear to regulate folate homeostasis differently from other engineered crops; AtGchI overexpression shifted the biosynthetic/breakdown pteridine ratio strongly toward biosynthetic species, but oxidized pteridines still accumulated substantially in some lines.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Pinto Saltillo (1424-FRI-026-120901/C); Pinto Café (wild variety); Pinto Durango (PT91082)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first step of para-aminobenzoate (PABA) synthesis in plastids for folate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous PvAdcs expression was slightly elevated in some AtGchI lines, consistent with possible stimulation of PABA production, but evidence was inconclusive.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvAdcs encodes the first enzymatic step of PABA synthesis, the second precursor branch of folate biosynthesis. 2. Selected AtGchI transgenic bean seeds showed modest PvAdcs transcript increases in some lines, while others were unchanged. 3. Increased PABA accumulation in transgenic seeds did not clearly correlate with PvAdcs expression, suggesting additional regulation beyond transcript abundance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in desiccated seeds of wild type and transgenic lines; slightly increased in S8, S34 and C25 relative to controls.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of endogenous PvAdcs in selected T4 seeds; PABA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme for the PABA branch, but not established here as the main bottleneck in bean seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed in desiccated seeds from AtGchI-expressing lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A logical stacking target with AtGchI to simultaneously increase both folate precursor branches in bean seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the main engineering target, PvAdcs was experimentally assessed in Results as a candidate explanation for unexpected PABA increases; the study suggests PABA enhancement could further raise folate when combined with high pteridine status.
